revision.text: #redirect[[en:Shirahige_Bridge]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

[[画像:Shirahigebashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都墨田区から。(2005年8月)]] '''白鬚橋'''（しらひげばし）は、[[隅田川]]にかかる[[橋]]で、[[東京都道306号王子千住南砂町線]]（[[明治通り (東京都)|明治通り]]）を通す。西岸は[[荒川区]]南千住三丁目と[[台東区]]橋場二丁目を分かち、東岸は[[墨田区]]堤通一丁目と二丁目を分かつ。橋名は東岸にある「白鬚神社」に因む。なお、白「'''髭'''」（くちひげの意）と誤記されることがあるが、白「'''鬚'''」（あごひげの意）が正しい。
創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは一三〇間（約230ｍ）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取ったが、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取り、[[関東大震災]]後の震災復興事業の一環として現在の橋に架け替えられることとなった。
もともと「[[橋場の渡し]]」と「[[白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
==橋の概要== *構造形式 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長 168.8ｍ *幅員 22.1ｍ *着工 [[昭和]]3年7月 *竣工 昭和6年8月8日 *施工主体 東京府 *橋梁設計 [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作 川崎造船所
==関連リンク== *[http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm 白鬚橋詳細データ]（[http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ 土木学会付属土木図書館][http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/page/report.html デジタルアーカイブス]より）
==隣の橋== （上流）－[[千住大橋]]－[[水神大橋]]－''白鬚橋''－[[桜橋]]－[[言問橋]]－（下流）
[[Category:日本の橋|しらひけはし]]

[[画像:Shirahigebashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都墨田区から。(2005年8月)]] '''白鬚橋'''（しらひげばし）は、[[隅田川]]にかかる[[橋]]で、[[東京都道306号王子千住南砂町線]]（[[明治通り (東京都)|明治通り]]）を通す。西岸は[[荒川区]]南千住三丁目と[[台東区]]橋場二丁目を分かち、東岸は[[墨田区]]堤通一丁目と二丁目を分かつ。橋名は東岸にある「白鬚神社」に因む。なお、白「'''髭'''」（くちひげの意）と誤記されることがあるが、白「'''鬚'''」（あごひげの意）が正しい。
創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは一三〇間（約230ｍ）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取ったが、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取り、[[関東大震災]]後の震災復興事業の一環として現在の橋に架け替えられることとなった。
もともと「[[橋場の渡し]]」と「[[白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
==橋の概要== *構造形式 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長 168.8ｍ *幅員 22.1ｍ *着工 [[昭和]]3年7月 *竣工 昭和6年8月8日 *施工主体 東京府 *橋梁設計 [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作 川崎造船所
==関連リンク== *[http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm 白鬚橋詳細データ]（[http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ 土木学会付属土木図書館][http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/page/report.html デジタルアーカイブス]より）
==隣の橋== （上流）－[[千住汐入大橋]]－[[水神大橋]]－''白鬚橋''－[[桜橋]]－[[言問橋]]－（下流）

[[画像:Shirahigebashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都墨田区から。(2005年8月)]] '''白鬚橋'''（しらひげばし）は、[[隅田川]]にかかる[[橋]]で、[[東京都道306号王子千住南砂町線]]（[[明治通り (東京都)|明治通り]]）を通す。西岸は[[荒川区]]南千住三丁目と[[台東区]]橋場二丁目を分かち、東岸は[[墨田区]]堤通一丁目と二丁目を分かつ。橋名は東岸にある「白鬚神社」に因む。なお、白「'''髭'''」（くちひげの意）と誤記されることがあるが、白「'''鬚'''」（あごひげの意）が正しい。
創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは一三〇間（約230ｍ）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取ったが、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取り、[[関東大震災]]後の震災復興事業の一環として現在の橋に架け替えられることとなった。
もともと「[[隅田川の渡し|橋場の渡し]]」と「[[隅田川の渡し|白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
==橋の概要== *構造形式: 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長: 168.8ｍ *幅員: 22.1ｍ *着工: [[昭和]]3年（1928年）7月 *竣工: 昭和6年（1931年）8月8日 *施工主体: 東京府 *橋梁設計: [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作: 川崎造船所
==関連リンク== *[http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm 白鬚橋詳細データ]（[http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ 土木学会付属土木図書館][http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/page/report.html デジタルアーカイブス]より）

創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは一三〇間（約230ｍ）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取ったが、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取り、[[関東大震災]]後の震災復興事業の一環として現在の橋に架け替えられることとなった。

[[画像:Shirahigebashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都墨田区から。(2005年8月)]] '''白鬚橋'''（しらひげばし）は、[[隅田川]]にかかる[[橋]]で、[[東京都道306号王子千住南砂町線]]（[[明治通り (東京都)|明治通り]]）を通す。西岸は[[荒川区]][[南千住 (荒川区)|南千住]]三丁目と[[台東区]]橋場二丁目を分かち、東岸は[[墨田区]]堤通一丁目と二丁目を分かつ。橋名は東岸にある「白鬚神社」に因む。なお、白「'''髭'''」（くちひげの意）と誤記されることがあるが、白「'''鬚'''」（あごひげの意）が正しい。
創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは一三〇間（約230ｍ）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取ったが、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取り、[[関東大震災]]後の震災復興事業の一環として現在の橋に架け替えられることとなった。
もともと「[[隅田川の渡し|橋場の渡し]]」と「[[隅田川の渡し|白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
==橋の概要== *構造形式: 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長: 168.8ｍ *幅員: 22.1ｍ *着工: [[昭和]]3年（1928年）7月 *竣工: 昭和6年（1931年）8月8日 *施工主体: 東京府 *橋梁設計: [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作: 川崎造船所
==出典== <references /> ==関連リンク== *[http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm 白鬚橋詳細データ] （[http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ 土木学会付属土木図書館][http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/page/report.html デジタルアーカイブス]より）

創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは一三〇間（約230m）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取ったが、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取り、[[関東大震災]]後の震災復興事業の一環として現在の橋に架け替えられることとなった。
もともと「[[隅田川の渡し|橋場の渡し]]」と「[[隅田川の渡し|白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。

もともと「[[隅田川の渡し|橋場の渡し]]」と「[[隅田川の渡し|白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
==橋の概要== *構造形式: 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長: 168.8m *幅員: 22.1m *着工: [[昭和]]3年（1928年）7月 *竣工: 昭和6年（1931年）8月8日 *施工主体: 東京府 *橋梁設計: [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作: 川崎造船所
== 路線バス == * [[都営バス]] ** [[都営バス南千住営業所#里22・南千47・南千48系統|里22]] [[日暮里駅]] - [[三河島駅]] - [[荒川区役所]] - 泪橋 - 橋場二丁目 - 東向島広小路 - [[亀戸駅]]
==隣の橋== （上流）－[[千住汐入大橋]]－[[水神大橋]]－'''白鬚橋'''－[[桜橋 (東京都)|桜橋]]－[[言問橋]]－（下流）

[[ファイル:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都荒川区から(2008年1月)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=東京|number=306|align=left}} [[ファイル:Shirahigebashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都墨田区から(2005年8月)]] '''白鬚橋'''（しらひげばし）は、[[隅田川]]にかかる[[橋]]で、[[東京都道306号王子千住南砂町線]]（[[明治通り (東京都)|明治通り]]）を通す。西岸は[[荒川区]][[南千住]]三丁目と[[台東区]]橋場二丁目を分かち、東岸は[[墨田区]]堤通一丁目と二丁目を分かつ。橋名は東岸にある「[[白鬚神社 (墨田区)|白鬚神社]]」に因む。なお、白「'''髭'''」（くちひげの意）と誤記されることがあるが、白「'''鬚'''」（あごひげの意）が正しい。
== 歴史 == 創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは一三〇間（約230m）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取ったが、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取り、[[関東大震災]]後の震災復興事業の一環として現在の橋に架け替えられることとなった。
もともと「[[隅田川の渡し|橋場の渡し]]」と「[[隅田川の渡し|白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
== 橋の概要 == *構造形式: 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長: 168.8m *幅員: 22.1m *着工: [[昭和]]3年（1928年）7月 *竣工: 昭和6年（1931年）8月8日 *施工主体: 東京府 *橋梁設計: [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作: 川崎造船所 *同様形式の橋：旭川市 旭橋
== 路線バス == * [[都営バス]] ** [[都営バス南千住営業所#里22・南千47・南千48系統|里22]] [[日暮里駅]] - [[三河島駅]] - [[荒川区役所]] - 泪橋 - 橋場二丁目 - 東向島広小路 - [[亀戸駅]]
== 隣の橋 == * [[隅田川]] : （上流） - [[千住汐入大橋]] - [[水神大橋]] - '''白鬚橋''' - [[桜橋 (東京都)|桜橋]] - [[言問橋]] - （下流）
== 関連項目 == *[[東京都の橋一覧]] *[[荒川アンダー ザ ブリッジ]]（当橋を数回、舞台にした漫画） <!--

{{隅田川の橋}} {{Coord|35|43|41|N|139|48|35|E|display=title|region:JP_type:landmark}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらひけはし}} [[Category:東京都区部の橋]] [[Category:荒川区の交通‎]] [[Category:南千住]] [[Category:墨田区の橋]] [[Category:台東区の橋]] [[Category:隅田川の橋]] [[Category:荒川区の建築物]]

[[ファイル:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都荒川区から(2008年1月)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=東京|number=306|align=left}} [[ファイル:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都墨田区から(2015年4月)]] '''白鬚橋'''（しらひげばし）は、[[隅田川]]にかかる[[橋]]で、[[東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線]]（[[明治通り (東京都)|明治通り]]）を通す。西岸は[[荒川区]][[南千住]]三丁目と[[台東区]]橋場二丁目を分かち、東岸は[[墨田区]]堤通一丁目と二丁目を分かつ。橋名は東岸にある「[[白鬚神社 (墨田区)|白鬚神社]]」に因む。なお、白「'''髭'''」（くちひげの意）と誤記されることがあるが、白「'''鬚'''」（あごひげの意）が正しい。
== 歴史 == 創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは一三〇間（約230m）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取ったが、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取り、[[関東大震災]]後の震災復興事業の一環として現在の橋に架け替えられることとなった。
もともと「[[隅田川の渡し|橋場の渡し]]」と「[[隅田川の渡し|白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
== 橋の概要 == *構造形式: 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長: 168.8m *幅員: 22.1m *着工: [[昭和]]3年（1928年）7月 *竣工: 昭和6年（1931年）8月8日 *施工主体: 東京府 *橋梁設計: [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作: 川崎造船所 *同様形式の橋：旭川市 旭橋
== 路線バス == * [[都営バス]] ** [[都営バス南千住営業所#里22・南千47・南千48系統|里22]] [[日暮里駅]] - [[三河島駅]] - [[荒川区役所]] - 泪橋 - 橋場二丁目 - 東向島広小路 - [[亀戸駅]]
== 隣の橋 == * [[隅田川]] : （上流） - [[千住汐入大橋]] - [[水神大橋]] - '''白鬚橋''' - [[桜橋 (東京都)|桜橋]] - [[言問橋]] - （下流）

revision.comment: ばしではありません はしです
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都荒川区から(2008年1月)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=東京|number=306|align=left}} [[ファイル:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都墨田区から(2015年4月)]] '''白鬚橋'''（しらひげばし）は、[[隅田川]]にかかる[[橋]]で、[[東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線]]（[[明治通り (東京都)|明治通り]]）を通す。西岸は[[荒川区]][[南千住]]三丁目と[[台東区]]橋場二丁目を分かち、東岸は[[墨田区]]堤通一丁目と二丁目を分かつ。橋名は東岸にある「[[白鬚神社 (墨田区)|白鬚神社]]」に因む。なお、白「'''髭'''」（くちひげの意）と誤記されることがあるが、白「'''鬚'''」（あごひげの意）が正しい。
はしなんですけど ばしではありません

revision.text:
[[ファイル:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都荒川区から(2008年1月)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=東京|number=306|align=left}} [[ファイル:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都墨田区から(2015年4月)]] [[ファイル:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|東京スカイツリーから(2016年4月)]] '''白鬚橋'''（しらひげばし）は、[[隅田川]]にかかる[[橋]]で、[[東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線]]（[[明治通り (東京都)|明治通り]]）を通す。西岸は[[荒川区]][[南千住]]三丁目と[[台東区]][[橋場]]二丁目を分かち、東岸は[[墨田区]][[堤通]]一丁目と二丁目を分かつ。橋名は東岸にある「[[白鬚神社 (墨田区)|白鬚神社]]」に因む。なお、白「'''髭'''」（くちひげの意）と誤記されることがあるが、白「'''鬚'''」（あごひげの意）が正しい表記である。
== 歴史 == 創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは一三〇間（約230m）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取った<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=橋から見た隅田川の歴史 |date=2002-06 |publisher=文芸社 |page=212 |isbn=4-8355-3894-3 |author=飯田雅男 |ref={{SfnRef|飯田|2002}}}}</ref>が、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取った。
大震災の被害にあったわけではないが、東京都の都市計画事業による一等大路第三類第16号線なる環状道路（現在の明治通り）の隅田川を横断する箇所ということで、鉄橋に架換えることとし、現在の橋に架け替えられることとなった<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=隅田川の橋 |date=1993-03 |publisher=墨田区立緑図書館 |page=61 |series=墨田区立図書館叢書8}}</ref>。
もともと「[[隅田川の渡し|橋場の渡し]]」と「[[隅田川の渡し|白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
== 橋の概要 == *構造形式: 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長: 168.8m *幅員: 22.1m *着工: [[昭和]]3年（1928年）7月 *竣工: 昭和6年（1931年）8月8日 *施工主体: 東京府 *橋梁設計: [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作: 川崎造船所 *同様形式の橋：[[旭橋 (旭川市) |旭橋]]
== 路線バス == * [[都営バス]] ** [[都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|里22]] [[日暮里駅]] - [[三河島駅]] - [[荒川区役所]] - 泪橋 - 橋場二丁目 - 東向島広小路 - [[亀戸駅]]

もともと「[[隅田川の渡し|橋場の渡し]]」と「[[隅田川の渡し|白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
== 橋の概要 == *構造形式: 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長: 167.6メートル{{Sfn|飯田|2002|p=212}} *幅員: 24.1メートル{{Sfn|飯田|2002|p=212}} *鋼材重量：1,923トン{{Sfn|飯田|2002|p=212}} *着工: [[昭和]]3年（1928年）7月 {{Sfn|飯田|2002|p=212}} *竣工: 昭和6年（1931年）6月 {{Sfn|飯田|2002|p=212}} *施工主体: 東京府 *橋梁設計: [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作: 川崎造船所 *同様形式の橋：[[旭橋 (旭川市) |旭橋]]
== 路線バス == * [[都営バス]] ** [[都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|里22]] [[日暮里駅]] - [[三河島駅]] - [[荒川区役所]] - 泪橋 - 橋場二丁目 - 東向島広小路 - [[亀戸駅]]
== 隣の橋 == * [[隅田川]] : （上流） - [[千住汐入大橋]] - [[水神大橋]] - '''白鬚橋''' - [[桜橋 (東京都)|桜橋]] - [[言問橋]] - （下流）

{{隅田川の橋}} {{Coord|35|43|41|N|139|48|35|E|display=title|region:JP_type:landmark}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらひけはし}} [[Category:東京都区部の橋]] [[Category:荒川区の交通]] [[Category:南千住]] [[Category:墨田区の橋]] [[Category:台東区の橋]] [[Category:隅田川の橋]] [[Category:東京都選定歴史的建造物]]

[[ファイル:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都荒川区から(2008年1月)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=東京|number=306|align=left}} [[ファイル:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都墨田区から(2015年4月)]] [[ファイル:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|東京スカイツリーから(2016年4月)]] '''白鬚橋'''（しらひげばし）は、[[隅田川]]にかかる[[橋]]で、[[東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線]]（[[明治通り (東京都)|明治通り]]）を通す。西岸は[[荒川区]][[南千住]]三丁目と[[台東区]][[橋場]]二丁目を分かち、東岸は[[墨田区]][[堤通]]一丁目と二丁目を分かつ。橋名は東岸にある「[[白鬚神社 (墨田区)|白鬚神社]]」に因む。なお、白「'''髭'''」（くちひげの意）と誤記されることがあるが、白「'''鬚'''」（あごひげの意）が正しい表記である。
== 歴史 == 創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは一三〇間（約230m）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取った<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=橋から見た隅田川の歴史 |date=2002-06 |publisher=文芸社 |page=212 |isbn=4-8355-3894-3 |author=飯田雅男 |ref={{SfnRef|飯田|2002}}}}</ref>が、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取った。
大震災の被害にあったわけではないが、東京都の都市計画事業による一等大路第三類第16号線なる環状道路（現在の明治通り）の隅田川を横断する箇所ということで、鉄橋に架換えることとし、現在の橋に架け替えられることとなった<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=隅田川の橋 |date=1993-03 |publisher=墨田区立緑図書館 |page=61 |series=墨田区立図書館叢書8}}</ref>。
もともと「[[隅田川の渡し|橋場の渡し]]」と「[[隅田川の渡し|白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
== 橋の概要 == *構造形式: 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長: 167.6メートル{{Sfn|飯田|2002|p=212}} *幅員: 24.1メートル{{Sfn|飯田|2002|p=212}} *鋼材重量：1,923トン{{Sfn|飯田|2002|p=212}} *着工: [[昭和]]3年（1928年）7月 {{Sfn|飯田|2002|p=212}} *竣工: 昭和6年（1931年）6月 {{Sfn|飯田|2002|p=212}} *施工主体: 東京府 *橋梁設計: [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作: 川崎造船所 *同様形式の橋：[[旭橋 (旭川市) |旭橋]]
== 路線バス == * [[都営バス]] ** [[都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|里22]] [[日暮里駅]] - [[三河島駅]] - [[荒川区役所]] - 泪橋 - 橋場二丁目 - 東向島広小路 - [[亀戸駅]]
== 作品 == * 早乙女勝元『美しい橋』<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=橋はかたる |date=1983-03 |publisher=墨田区教育委員会社会教育課 |page=13 |isbn=}}</ref>
== 隣の橋 == * [[隅田川]] : （上流） - [[千住汐入大橋]] - [[水神大橋]] - '''白鬚橋''' - [[桜橋 (東京都)|桜橋]] - [[言問橋]] - （下流）

revision.comment: Created page with "[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]] '''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo..."
revision.text:
[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine (Sumida Ward)|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * **Structure type**: Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * **Length**: 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * **Width**: 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * **Steel weight**: 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * **Construction started**: Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * **Completed**: Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * **Contractor**: Tokyo Prefecture * **Designer**: {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * **Bridge girder manufacturer**: Kawasaki Dockyard * **Similar design**: {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}

'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine (Sumida Ward)|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>

The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.

'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine (Sumida Ward)|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|ja|都営バス}}''' - {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|ja|日暮里駅}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|ja|三河島駅}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} - '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋}} - (Downstream)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakawa Under the Bridge|ja|荒川アンダー ザ ブリッジ}} (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)

{{Sumida River Bridges}} {{Coord|35|43|41|N|139|48|35|E|display=title|region:JP_type:landmark}}
[[Category:Bridges in Tokyo]] [[Category:Transport in Arakawa Ward]] [[Category:Minami-Senju]] [[Category:Bridges in Sumida Ward]] [[Category:Bridges in Taitō Ward]] [[Category:Sumida River bridges]] [[Category:Selected Historic Buildings in Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:白鬚橋]]

[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine (Sumida Ward)|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|ja|都営バス}}''' - {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|ja|日暮里駅}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|ja|三河島駅}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} - '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋}} - (Downstream)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakawa Under the Bridge|ja|荒川アンダー ザ ブリッジ}} (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)

Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.

[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine (Sumida Ward)|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}

[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine (Sumida Ward)|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|ja|都営バス}}''' - {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|ja|日暮里駅}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|ja|三河島駅}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} - '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋}} - (Downstream)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakawa Under the Bridge|ja|荒川アンダー ザ ブリッジ}} (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)

The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine (Sumida Ward)|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>

The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.

[[Category:Bridges in Tokyo]] [[Category:Transport in Arakawa Ward]] [[Category:Minami-Senju]] [[Category:Bridges in Sumida Ward]] [[Category:Bridges in Taitō Ward]] [[Category:Sumida River bridges]] [[Category:Selected Historic Buildings in Tokyo]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shirahige Bridge]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Shirahige Bridge]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:白鬚橋]]

[[ファイル:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都荒川区から(2008年1月)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=東京|number=306|align=left}} [[ファイル:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都墨田区から(2015年4月)]] [[ファイル:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|東京スカイツリーから(2016年4月)]] '''白鬚橋'''（しらひげばし）は、[[隅田川]]にかかる[[橋]]で、[[東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線]]（[[明治通り (東京都)|明治通り]]）を通す。西岸は[[荒川区]][[南千住]]三丁目と[[台東区]][[橋場]]二丁目を分かち、東岸は[[墨田区]][[堤通]]一丁目と二丁目を分かつ。橋名は東岸にある「[[白鬚神社 (墨田区)|白鬚神社]]」に因む。なお、白「'''髭'''」（くちひげの意）と誤記されることがあるが、白「'''鬚'''」（あごひげの意）が正しい表記である。
== 歴史 == 創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは130間（約230メートル）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取った<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|和書 |title=橋から見た隅田川の歴史 |date=2002-06 |publisher=文芸社 |page=212 |isbn=4-8355-3894-3 |author=飯田雅男 |ref={{SfnRef|飯田|2002}}}}</ref>が、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取った。
[[関東大震災]]の被害にあったわけではないが、東京都の都市計画事業による一等大路第三類第16号線なる環状道路（現在の明治通り）の隅田川を横断する箇所ということで、現在の鉄橋に架け替えられることとなった<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=隅田川の橋 |date=1993-03 |publisher=墨田区立緑図書館 |page=61 |series=墨田区立図書館叢書8}}</ref>。
もともと「[[隅田川の渡し|橋場の渡し]]」と「[[隅田川の渡し|白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
== 橋の概要 == *構造形式: 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長: 167.6メートル<ref name=":0" /> *幅員: 24.1メートル<ref name=":0" /> *鋼材重量：1,923トン<ref name=":0" /> *着工: [[昭和]]3年（1928年）7月<ref name=":0" /> *竣工: 昭和6年（1931年）6月<ref name=":0" /> *施工主体: 東京府 *橋梁設計: [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作: 川崎造船所 *同様形式の橋：[[旭橋 (旭川市) |旭橋]]
== 路線バス == * [[都営バス]] ** [[都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|里22]] [[日暮里駅]] - [[三河島駅]] - [[荒川区役所]] - 泪橋 - 橋場二丁目 - 東向島広小路 - [[亀戸駅]]
== 作品 == * [[早乙女勝元]]『美しい橋』<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=橋はかたる |date=1983-03 |publisher=墨田区教育委員会社会教育課 |page=13 |isbn=}}</ref>
== 隣の橋 == * [[隅田川]] : （上流） - [[千住汐入大橋]] - [[水神大橋]] - '''白鬚橋''' - [[桜橋 (東京都)|桜橋]] - [[言問橋]] - （下流）
== 関連項目 == *[[荒川アンダー ザ ブリッジ]]（当橋を数回、舞台にした漫画）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm 白鬚橋詳細データ]（[http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ 土木学会付属土木図書館][http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/page/report.html デジタルアーカイブス]より）
{{隅田川の橋}} {{Coord|35|43|41|N|139|48|35|E|display=title|region:JP_type:landmark}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらひけはし}} [[Category:東京都区部の橋]] [[Category:荒川区の交通]] [[Category:南千住]] [[Category:墨田区の橋]] [[Category:台東区の橋]] [[Category:隅田川の橋]] [[Category:東京都選定歴史的建造物]]

[[ファイル:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都荒川区から(2008年1月)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=東京|number=306|align=left}} [[ファイル:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|東京都墨田区から(2015年4月)]] [[ファイル:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|東京スカイツリーから(2016年4月)]] '''白鬚橋'''（しらひげばし）は、[[隅田川]]にかかる[[橋]]で、[[東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線]]（[[明治通り (東京都)|明治通り]]）を通す。西岸は[[荒川区]][[南千住]]三丁目と[[台東区]][[橋場]]二丁目を分かち、東岸は[[墨田区]][[堤通]]一丁目と二丁目を分かつ。橋名は東岸にある「[[白鬚神社 (墨田区)|白鬚神社]]」に因む。なお、白「'''髭'''」（くちひげの意）と誤記されることがあるが、白「'''鬚'''」（あごひげの意）が正しい表記である。
== 歴史 == 創架は[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）5月。近在の人々が基金を募って資本金を作り「白鬚橋株式会社」を設立、大正2年4月に着工して約一年で完成した木橋である。長さは130間（約230メートル）であったと伝えられる。橋に番小屋を置き、大人一人1銭の通行料を取った<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|和書 |title=橋から見た隅田川の歴史 |date=2002-06 |publisher=文芸社 |page=212 |isbn=4-8355-3894-3 |author=飯田雅男 |ref={{SfnRef|飯田|2002}}}}</ref>が、当時は渡し舟も多く走り、経営は苦しかったという。後に2銭に値上げするも間に合わず橋の維持に支障をきたすようになり、[[1925年]]（大正14年）に東京府が買い取った。
[[関東大震災]]の被害に遭ったわけでは無いが、復興計画にあたって一等大路第三類第16号線（現在の明治通り）の隅田川を横断する箇所ということで、現在の鉄橋に架け替えられることとなった<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=隅田川の橋 |date=1993-03 |publisher=墨田区立緑図書館 |page=61 |series=墨田区立図書館叢書8}}</ref>。
もともと「[[隅田川の渡し|橋場の渡し]]」と「[[隅田川の渡し|白鬚の渡し]]」という渡船場があった場所であり、特に橋場の渡しは[[江戸時代]]、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[千住大橋]]が完成して主だった街道筋が移されるまでは、隅田川を渡る中心地であった。[[伊勢物語]]での東下りの中で主人公（[[在原業平]]と同一視される）が有名な「言問」の歌を詠んだのはこの渡しとされる。
== 橋の概要 == *構造形式: 下路式ブレースドリブドタイド[[アーチ橋]] *橋長: 167.6メートル<ref name=":0" /> *幅員: 24.1メートル<ref name=":0" /> *鋼材重量：1,923トン<ref name=":0" /> *着工: [[昭和]]3年（1928年）7月<ref name=":0" /> *竣工: 昭和6年（1931年）6月<ref name=":0" /> *施工主体: 東京府 *橋梁設計: [[増田淳]] *橋桁製作: 川崎造船所 *同様形式の橋：[[旭橋 (旭川市) |旭橋]]
== 路線バス == * [[都営バス]] ** [[都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|里22]] [[日暮里駅]] - [[三河島駅]] - [[荒川区役所]] - 泪橋 - 橋場二丁目 - 東向島広小路 - [[亀戸駅]]
== 作品 == * [[早乙女勝元]]『美しい橋』<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=橋はかたる |date=1983-03 |publisher=墨田区教育委員会社会教育課 |page=13 |isbn=}}</ref>
== 隣の橋 == * [[隅田川]] : （上流） - [[千住汐入大橋]] - [[水神大橋]] - '''白鬚橋''' - [[桜橋 (東京都)|桜橋]] - [[言問橋]] - （下流）
== 関連項目 == *[[荒川アンダー ザ ブリッジ]]（当橋を数回、舞台にした漫画）

revision.text:
[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine (Sumida Ward)|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|ja|都営バス}}''' - {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|ja|日暮里駅}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|ja|三河島駅}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} - '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋}} - (Downstream)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakawa Under the Bridge|ja|荒川アンダー ザ ブリッジ}} (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm Detailed Data on Shirahige Bridge] (via the [http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ Japan Society of Civil Engineers Digital Library])
{{Sumida River Bridges}} {{Coord|35|43|41|N|139|48|35|E|display=title|region:JP_type:landmark}}

[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}

== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>

'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).

A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}

'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|ja|都営バス}}''' - {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|ja|日暮里駅}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|ja|三河島駅}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} - '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋}} - (Downstream)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Arakawa Under the Bridge|ja|荒川アンダー ザ ブリッジ}} (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)
== References == {{Reflist}}

[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}

'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|ja|都営バス}}''' - {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|ja|日暮里駅}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|ja|三河島駅}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} - '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋}} - (Downstream)
== See Also == * {{ill|Arakawa Under the Bridge|ja|荒川アンダー ザ ブリッジ}} (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm Detailed Data on Shirahige Bridge] (via the [http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ Japan Society of Civil Engineers Digital Library])

[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|ja|都営バス}}''' - {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|ja|日暮里駅}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|ja|三河島駅}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} - '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋}} - (Downstream)
== See Also == * {{ill|Arakawa Under the Bridge|ja|荒川アンダー ザ ブリッジ}} (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)

'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|ja|都営バス}}''' - {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|ja|日暮里駅}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|ja|三河島駅}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} - '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋}} - (Downstream)
== See Also == * {{ill|Arakawa Under the Bridge|ja|荒川アンダー ザ ブリッジ}} (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)

[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring aqqroximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|ja|都営バス}}''' - {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統}}: {{ill|Niqqori Station|ja|日暮里駅}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|ja|三河島駅}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|ja|亀戸駅}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋}} - '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋}} - (Downstream)

[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|ja|隅田川}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|ja|白鬚神社}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring a11roximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|Tales of Ise|ja|伊勢物語}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|ja|在原業平}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)}}
