<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580501]] Wikidata name: Shirahige Bridge [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]], carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Road Route 306|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525778}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Senju|1=Minami-Senju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407548}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba|1=Hashiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543765}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuijidō|1=Tsutsumi-Dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428012}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby [[:en:Shirahige Shrine|Shirahige Shrine]]. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba Ferry|1=Hashiba Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543767}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Ferry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Bridge|1=Senju-ōhashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405344}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the [[:en:The Tales of Ise|Tales of Ise]], attributed to [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]], was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun Masuda|1=Jun Masuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429345}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|1=Asahi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511817}}
== Public Transportation == * '''[[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]]''' ** {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Route 22|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}}: [[:en:Nippori Station|Ni11ori Station]] - [[:en:Mikawashima Station|Mikawashima Station]] - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa Ward Office|1=Arakawa Ward Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618538}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - [[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]] : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Shiomi Bridge|1=Senju Shioiri Ōhashi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405350}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|1=Sakura Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538116}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoi Bridge|1=Kototoi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630974}} - (Downstream) == See Also == * [[:en:Arakawa Under the Bridge|Arakawa Under the Bridge]] (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580501]] Wikidata name: Shirahige Bridge [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]], carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Road Route 306|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525778}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Senju|1=Minami-Senju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407548}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba|1=Hashiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543765}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuijidō|1=Tsutsumi-Dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428012}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby [[:en:Shirahige Shrine|Shirahige Shrine]]. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba Ferry|1=Hashiba Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543767}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Ferry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Bridge|1=Senju-ōhashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405344}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the [[:en:The Tales of Ise|Tales of Ise]], attributed to [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]], was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun Masuda|1=Jun Masuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429345}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|1=Asahi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511817}}
== Public Transportation == * '''[[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]]''' ** {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Route 22|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}}: [[:en:Nippori Station|Ni11ori Station]] - [[:en:Mikawashima Station|Mikawashima Station]] - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa Ward Office|1=Arakawa Ward Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618538}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - [[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]] : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Shiomi Bridge|1=Senju Shioiri Ōhashi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405350}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|1=Sakura Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538116}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoi Bridge|1=Kototoi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630974}} - (Downstream) == See Also == * [[:en:Arakawa Under the Bridge|Arakawa Under the Bridge]] (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm Detailed Data on Shirahige Bridge] (via the [http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ Japan Society of Civil Engineers Digital Library])

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580501]] Wikidata name: Shirahige Bridge [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]], carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Road Route 306|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525778}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Senju|1=Minami-Senju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407548}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba|1=Hashiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543765}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuijidō|1=Tsutsumi-Dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428012}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby [[:en:Shirahige Shrine|Shirahige Shrine]]. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba Ferry|1=Hashiba Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543767}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Ferry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Bridge|1=Senju-ōhashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405344}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the [[:en:The Tales of Ise|Tales of Ise]], attributed to [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]], was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun Masuda|1=Jun Masuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429345}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|1=Asahi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511817}}
== Public Transportation == * '''[[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]]''' ** {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Route 22|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}}: [[:en:Nippori Station|Ni11ori Station]] - [[:en:Mikawashima Station|Mikawashima Station]] - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa Ward Office|1=Arakawa Ward Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618538}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - [[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]] : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Shiomi Bridge|1=Senju Shioiri Ōhashi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405350}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|1=Sakura Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538116}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoi Bridge|1=Kototoi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630974}} - (Downstream) == See Also == * [[:en:Arakawa Under the Bridge|Arakawa Under the Bridge]] (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm Detailed Data on Shirahige Bridge] (via the [http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ Japan Society of Civil Engineers Digital Library])

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580501]] Wikidata name: Shirahige Bridge [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]], carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Road Route 306|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525778}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Senju|1=Minami-Senju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407548}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba|1=Hashiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543765}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuijidō|1=Tsutsumi-Dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428012}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby [[:en:Shirahige Shrine|Shirahige Shrine]]. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba Ferry|1=Hashiba Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543767}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Ferry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Bridge|1=Senju-ōhashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405344}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the [[:en:The Tales of Ise|Tales of Ise]], attributed to [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]], was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun Masuda|1=Jun Masuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429345}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|1=Asahi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511817}}
== Public Transportation == * '''[[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]]''' ** {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Route 22|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}}: [[:en:Nippori Station|Ni11ori Station]] - [[:en:Mikawashima Station|Mikawashima Station]] - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa Ward Office|1=Arakawa Ward Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618538}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - [[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]] : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Shiomi Bridge|1=Senju Shioiri Ōhashi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405350}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|1=Sakura Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538116}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoi Bridge|1=Kototoi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630974}} - (Downstream) == See Also == * [[:en:Arakawa Under the Bridge|Arakawa Under the Bridge]] (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm Detailed Data on Shirahige Bridge] (via the [http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ Japan Society of Civil Engineers Digital Library])

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580501]] Wikidata name: Shirahige Bridge [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]], carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Road Route 306|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525778}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Senju|1=Minami-Senju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407548}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba|1=Hashiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543765}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuijidō|1=Tsutsumi-Dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428012}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby [[:en:Shirahige Shrine|Shirahige Shrine]]. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba Ferry|1=Hashiba Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543767}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Ferry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Bridge|1=Senju-ōhashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405344}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the [[:en:The Tales of Ise|Tales of Ise]], attributed to [[:en:Ariwara no Narihira|Ariwara no Narihira]], was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun Masuda|1=Jun Masuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429345}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|1=Asahi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511817}}
== Public Transportation == * '''[[:en:Toei Bus|Toei Bus]]''' ** {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Route 22|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}}: [[:en:Nippori Station|Ni11ori Station]] - [[:en:Mikawashima Station|Mikawashima Station]] - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa Ward Office|1=Arakawa Ward Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618538}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - [[:en:Kameido Station|Kameido Station]]
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * [[:en:Sumida River|Sumida River]] : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Shiomi Bridge|1=Senju Shioiri Ōhashi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405350}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|1=Sakura Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538116}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoi Bridge|1=Kototoi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630974}} - (Downstream) == See Also == * [[:en:Arakawa Under the Bridge|Arakawa Under the Bridge]] (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm Detailed Data on Shirahige Bridge] (via the [http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ Japan Society of Civil Engineers Digital Library])

[[ja:白鬚橋]]
<!--Shirahige Bridge--><!--Shirahige Bridge--><!--Shirahige Bridge--><!--Shirahige Bridge--><!--Shirahige Bridge--><!--Shirahige Bridge--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shirahigebashi}} {{wikidata link|Q11580501}} [[Category:Bridges in Sumida, Tokyo]] [[Category:Bridges in Taito, Tokyo]] [[Category:Bridges in the special wards of Tokyo]] [[Category:Bridges in Tokyo]] [[Category:Bridges over the Sumida River]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Transport in Arakawa, Tokyo]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580501]] Wikidata name: Shirahige Bridge [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|en|Sumida River|WD=Q222149|lt=Sumida River}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Road Route 306|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525778}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Senju|1=Minami-Senju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407548}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba|1=Hashiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543765}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuijidō|1=Tsutsumi-Dōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428012}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|en|Shirahige Shrine|WD=Q705303|lt=Shirahige Shrine}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba Ferry|1=Hashiba Ferry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543767}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Ferry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Bridge|1=Senju-ōhashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405344}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|The Tales of Ise|en|The Tales of Ise|WD=Q1539995|lt=Tales of Ise}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|en|Ariwara no Narihira|WD=Q451991|lt=Ariwara no Narihira}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun Masuda|1=Jun Masuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429345}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|1=Asahi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511817}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|en|Toei Bus|WD=Q859227|lt=Toei Bus}}''' ** {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Route 22|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|en|Nippori Station|WD=Q801262|lt=Ni11ori Station}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|en|Mikawashima Station|WD=Q865170|lt=Mikawashima Station}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa Ward Office|1=Arakawa Ward Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618538}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|en|Kameido Station|WD=Q1036376|lt=Kameido Station}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|en|Sumida River|WD=Q222149|lt=Sumida River}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Shiomi Bridge|1=Senju Shioiri Ōhashi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405350}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|1=Sakura Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538116}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoi Bridge|1=Kototoi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630974}} - (Downstream) == See Also == * {{ill|Arakawa Under the Bridge|en|Arakawa Under the Bridge|WD=Q625474|lt=Arakawa Under the Bridge}} (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm Detailed Data on Shirahige Bridge] (via the [http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ Japan Society of Civil Engineers Digital Library])

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]]
[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|en|Sumida River|WD=Q222149|lt=Sumida River|1=Sumida River}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Road Route 306|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525778}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Senju|1=Minami-Senju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407548}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba|1=Hashiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543765}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuijidō|1=Tsutsumidōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428012}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|en|Shirahige Shrine|WD=Q705303|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba Ferry|1=Q11543767|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543767}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Ferry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Bridge|1=Senju-ōhashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405344}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|The Tales of Ise|en|The Tales of Ise|WD=Q1539995|lt=Tales of Ise|1=The Tales of Ise}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|en|Ariwara no Narihira|WD=Q451991|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|1=Ariwara no Narihira}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun Masuda|1=Jun Masuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429345}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|1=Asahi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511817}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|en|Toei Bus|WD=Q859227|lt=Toei Bus|1=Toei Bus}}''' ** {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Route 22|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|en|Nippori Station|WD=Q801262|lt=Ni11ori Station|1=Nippori Station}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|en|Mikawashima Station|WD=Q865170|lt=Mikawashima Station|1=Mikawashima Station}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa Ward Office|1=Q11618538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618538}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|en|Kameido Station|WD=Q1036376|lt=Kameido Station|1=Kameido Station}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|en|Sumida River|WD=Q222149|lt=Sumida River|1=Sumida River}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Shiomi Bridge|1=Senju Shioiri Ōhashi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405350}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|1=Sakura Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538116}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoi Bridge|1=Kototoi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630974}} - (Downstream) == See Also == * {{ill|Arakawa Under the Bridge|en|Arakawa Under the Bridge|WD=Q625474|lt=Arakawa Under the Bridge|1=Arakawa Under the Bridge}} (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://library.jsce.or.jp/jscelib/committee/2003/bridge/A5-027.htm Detailed Data on Shirahige Bridge] (via the [http://www.jsce.or.jp/library/ Japan Society of Civil Engineers Digital Library])

== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|en|Toei Bus|WD=Q859227|lt=Toei Bus|1=Toei Bus}}''' ** {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Route 22|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|en|Nippori Station|WD=Q801262|lt=Ni11ori Station|1=Nippori Station}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|en|Mikawashima Station|WD=Q865170|lt=Mikawashima Station|1=Mikawashima Station}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa Ward Office|1=Q11618538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618538}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|en|Kameido Station|WD=Q1036376|lt=Kameido Station|1=Kameido Station}}

<!--Shirahige Bridge--><!--Shirahige Bridge--><!--Shirahige Bridge--><!--Shirahige Bridge--><!--Shirahige Bridge--><!--Shirahige Bridge--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shirahigebashi}} {{wikidata link|Q11580501}} [[Category:Bridges in Sumida, Tokyo]] [[Category:Bridges in Taito, Tokyo]] [[Category:Bridges in the special wards of Tokyo]] [[Category:Bridges in Tokyo]] [[Category:Bridges over the Sumida River]] [[Category:Tokyo Selected Historical Buildings]] [[Category:Transport in Arakawa, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Shirahigebashi]][[ja:白鬚橋]]
[[File:Shirahige bashi.jpg|thumb|200px|From Arakawa Ward, Tokyo (January 2008)]] {{Ja_Pref_Route_Sign|pref=Tokyo|number=306|align=left}} [[File:Shirahigebashi-sundown-side-april28-2015.jpg|thumb|200px|From Sumida Ward, Tokyo (April 2015)]] [[File:Shirahigebashi-fromskytree-2016 4 9.jpg|thumb|200px|From Tokyo Skytree (April 2016)]]
'''Shirahige Bridge''' (白鬚橋, ''Shirahige-bashi'') is a bridge spanning the {{ill|Sumida River|en|Sumida River|WD=Q222149|lt=Sumida River|1=Sumida River}}, carrying the {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|ja|東京都道306号王子千住夢の島線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Route 306|1=Tokyo Metropolitan Road Route 306|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11525778}} (commonly known as Meiji-dōri). The western bank separates {{ill|Minami-Senju|ja|南千住|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-Senju|1=Minami-Senju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407548}}, Arakawa Ward, from {{ill|Hashiba|ja|橋場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba|1=Hashiba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543765}}, Taitō Ward, while the eastern bank divides {{ill|Tsuijidō|ja|堤通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuijidō|1=Tsutsumidōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428012}} 1 and 2-chōme in Sumida Ward. The bridge takes its name from the nearby {{ill|Shirahige Shrine|en|Shirahige Shrine|WD=Q705303|lt=Shirahige Shrine|1=Shirahige Shrine}}. Note that the correct kanji for the name is "鬚" (chin whiskers), often mistakenly written as "髭" (moustache).
== History == The bridge was first constructed in May 1914 (Taishō 3). Local residents raised funds to establish the Shirahige Bridge Corporation, beginning construction in April 1913 (Taishō 2). The wooden bridge was completed within a year, measuring approximately 130 ken (about 230 meters) in length. A toll of one sen per person was charged<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=The History of the Sumida River as Seen from Its Bridges|author=飯田雅男|publisher=Bungeisha|date=2002-06|page=212|isbn=4-8355-3894-3}}</ref>, but competition from ferry services made it challenging to maintain operations. Despite doubling the toll to two sen, the company struggled to maintain the bridge, eventually selling it to Tokyo Prefecture in 1925 (Taishō 14).
Although it suffered no damage during the {{ill|1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923 Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, the urban planning project for the city's first-class ring road (Route No. 16, now Meiji-dōri) necessitated replacing the wooden bridge with the current iron bridge.<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Bridges of Sumida River|publisher=Sumida Ward Green Library|date=1993-03|series=Sumida Ward Library Series 8|page=61}}</ref>
The location was originally home to the "{{ill|Hashiba Ferry|ja|橋場の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashiba Ferry|1=Q11543767|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543767}}" and "{{ill|Shirahige Ferry|ja|白鬚の渡し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirahige Ferry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," with Hashiba Ferry serving as a primary crossing point on the Sumida River during the Edo period until the construction of {{ill|Senju Bridge|ja|千住大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Bridge|1=Senju-ōhashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405344}} in Bunroku 3 (1594). The famous "Kototoi no Uta" poem from the {{ill|The Tales of Ise|en|The Tales of Ise|WD=Q1539995|lt=Tales of Ise|1=The Tales of Ise}}, attributed to {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|en|Ariwara no Narihira|WD=Q451991|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|1=Ariwara no Narihira}}, was composed at this crossing.
== Bridge Specifications == * '''Structure type''': Through-arch braced ribbed bridge * '''Length''': 167.6 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Width''': 24.1 meters<ref name=":0" /> * '''Steel weight''': 1,923 tons<ref name=":0" /> * '''Construction started''': Shōwa 3 (1928) July<ref name=":0" /> * '''Completed''': Shōwa 6 (1931) June<ref name=":0" /> * '''Contractor''': Tokyo Prefecture * '''Designer''': {{ill|Jun Masuda|ja|増田淳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun Masuda|1=Jun Masuda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429345}} * '''Bridge girder manufacturer''': Kawasaki Dockyard * '''Similar design''': {{ill|Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|ja|旭橋 (旭川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Bridge (Asahikawa)|1=Asahi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11511817}}
== Public Transportation == * '''{{ill|Toei Bus|en|Toei Bus|WD=Q859227|lt=Toei Bus|1=Toei Bus}}''' ** {{ill|Route 22|ja|都営バス南千住営業所#里22系統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Route 22|1=Toei Bus Minami-Senju Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643502}}: {{ill|Nippori Station|en|Nippori Station|WD=Q801262|lt=Ni11ori Station|1=Nippori Station}} - {{ill|Mikawashima Station|en|Mikawashima Station|WD=Q865170|lt=Mikawashima Station|1=Mikawashima Station}} - {{ill|Arakawa Ward Office|ja|荒川区役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa Ward Office|1=Q11618538|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618538}} - Namidabashi - Hashiba 2-chōme - Higashi Mukōjima Hirokōji - {{ill|Kameido Station|en|Kameido Station|WD=Q1036376|lt=Kameido Station|1=Kameido Station}}
== Cultural References == * Sōtome Katsumoto's ''Beautiful Bridge'' (美しい橋)<ref>{{Cite book|title=The Stories of Bridges|publisher=Sumida Ward Education Committee|date=1983-03|page=13}}</ref>
== Nearby Bridges == * {{ill|Sumida River|en|Sumida River|WD=Q222149|lt=Sumida River|1=Sumida River}} : (Upstream) - {{ill|Senju Shiomi Bridge|ja|千住汐入大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senju Shiomi Bridge|1=Senju Shioiri Ōhashi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405350}} - {{ill|Suijin Bridge|ja|水神大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin Bridge|qq=}} * '''Shirahige Bridge''' - {{ill|Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|ja|桜橋 (東京都)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura Bridge (Tokyo)|1=Sakura Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538116}} - {{ill|Kototoi Bridge|ja|言問橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoi Bridge|1=Kototoi Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630974}} - (Downstream) == See Also == * {{ill|Arakawa Under the Bridge|en|Arakawa Under the Bridge|WD=Q625474|lt=Arakawa Under the Bridge|1=Arakawa Under the Bridge}} (A manga that features this bridge in several scenes)
